explanation: This command uses the original syntax of the Sigma rule and matches the detection criteria. It is a direct representation of the rule's expected behavior.
The insertion of an escape character before the second '/' in '/C' may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly normalize command-line arguments.
Appending a quote at the end of the command line may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly handle trailing characters.
Adding extra spaces at the end of the command line may bypass detection if the SIEM does not properly trim whitespace.
Lowercase substitution of flags may bypass detection if the SIEM does not normalize case for command-line arguments.
Reordering the flags may bypass detection if the SIEM does not account for argument order in its parsing logic.
